message.content: Explain what a YAML frontmatter block is in a markdown file and when to use it.

YAML frontmatter is a metadata block at the very top of a markdown file, fenced by `---` on its own lines. It holds structured key-value data like title, tags, dates, and custom fields. Static site generators and note systems (Jekyll, Hugo, Obsidian) parse it to drive templating and indexing.

Use it whenever a markdown document needs typed metadata separate from the narrative body.
Give me a minimal example for an Obsidian-style note.

```
---
title: My Note
tags: [writing, reference]
created: 2026-04-16
---

# My Note

Body goes here.
```
